title: Delta Rises on Profit Guidance; EasyJet Moves on Apollo Bid | Stock Movers
title_cn: 达美航空因盈利指引而上涨；易捷航空 (EasyJet) 继续竞标阿波罗 (Apollo)库存搬运工
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers:

- Delta (DAL) shares are higher after it reaffirmed its full-year profit guidance, citing strong demand for premium, corporate and international travel that helped offset the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history. The airline earned an adjusted $1.56 a share in the second quarter, topping analysts’ estimates, with revenue rising 14% from a year earlier and capacity increasing just 1%.
- EasyJet (EZJ) shares are moving on news it received a fresh offer from Apollo Global Management for 715 pence a share, beating a rival proposal from Castlelake. EasyJet is "no longer minded to recommend the Castlelake proposal" and would be minded to recommend Apollo's bid to its shareholders, given that Apollo's £5.7 billion bid is superior to Castlelake's £5.5 billion offer.
- WD-40 (WDFC) shares jumped after the lubricant spray maker boosted its net sales forecast for the full year. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EasyJet Gets £5.7 Billion Apollo Offer in Possible Bidding War

summary: EasyJet Plc received an offer from private equity firm Apollo Global Management for 715 pence a share that beats a rival proposal from Castlelake LP, a surprise twist in the takeover saga that sets up a possible bidding war between the two US investment funds.

title: Americans Are Stuck in a Rut — And a Lack of Fun Might Be to Blame
title_cn: 美国人陷入墨守成规——缺乏乐趣可能是罪魁祸首
summary:
America appears to be suffering from a fun shortage. From the rising cost of concert tickets, to bowling alley and nightclub closures, to venue shortages for kids to play in local sports leagues, the fun deficit is real and it's making Americans more isolated and lonelier than previous generations.

Bloomberg's Ben Steverman joins Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to explore some easy solutions to bring fun back into our everyday lives. (Source: Bloomberg)

He says JPMorgan Chase struck the right balance on the SK Hynix pricing. Griggs speaks to Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow in New York at the Nasdaq.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Hynix ADRs Surge After Record $26.5 Billion US Offering
title_cn: SK 海力士 ADR 在美国发行创纪录的 265 亿美元后飙升
summary:
SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts surged above their offering price, after the South Korean memory chipmaker raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company.

The ADRs opened at $170 each on Friday in New York, versus an offering price of $149 apiece. The successful mega-listing brushed aside concern over recent volatility among chipmakers, demonstrating investors have plenty of appetite for direct exposure to SK Hynix’s dominant position in high-bandwidth memory for artificial intelligence computing. The ADRs are trading at a premium of about 17% over the closing price of the Seoul-listed common stock, due in part to restrictions on exchanging the South Korean stock for the US instruments.

We get reaction from Anthony Hughes, Bloomberg News US ECM Reporter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Eases Export Curbs on UAE, Opening Door for AI Chip Sales
title_cn: 美国放宽对阿联酋的出口限制，为人工智能芯片销售打开大门

title: SK Chairman Chey Tae-won on SK Hynix Debut, AI Demand and US Plans

The head of the South Korean chipmaker talks about raising $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company and also discusses the outlook for AI demand and his US investment plans. summary: SK Hynix's US debut is off to a strong start, with its ADRs indicated to open above the offering price. Di Zhou, portfolio manager at Thornburg Investment Management, says, "We're currently in a memory supercycle. Fundamentals are very strong, and SK Hynix is one of the highest-quality pure-play memory companies globally." She speaks on Bloomberg Open Interest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Chairman Has 'Some Confidence' Demand Will Grow

title: Government Action is Crucial for AI Entrepreneurs Says Pelgo CEO
title_cn: Pelgo 首席执行官表示，政府行动对人工智能企业家至关重要
summary:
Artificial intelligence could reshape the jobs of nearly 80 million people across Southeast Asia, but there is little sign it has led to large-scale employment cuts, according to the International Labour Organization.
The ILO urged governments to strengthen AI governance and better integrate AI policies through a human-centered approach to help workers and businesses adapt.
Pelgo CEO Chieh Huang discusses how AI is impacting the labor market and how it could serve as a tool for displaced workers rather than a job eliminator. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Bust Fallout Would Be 'More Significant' Than Dot-Com, Says George Noble
title_cn: 乔治·诺布尔 (George Noble) 表示，人工智能泡沫破灭的影响将比互联网泡沫“更严重”
summary:
The US stock market’s rally to new highs has been propelled by so few stocks that it’s reminding some on Wall Street of the runup to the dot-com bubble.
George Noble, Managing Partner of Noble Capital Advisors, discusses the AI trade and how he believes the coming bust would be much worse than the dot-com bubble fallout. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Chairman Calls SK Hynix US Debut a 'Dream Come True'
title_cn: SK董事长称SK海力士在美国的首次亮相是“梦想成真”

title: Semiconductor Stocks Are Overbought, Morgan Stanley's Shalett Warns

summary: Lisa Shalett, Morgan Stanley Wealth Management CIO, discusses the current state of the semiconductor sector, highlighting that chip stocks have become meaningfully overbought with some indexes up nearly 100% year-to-date before recent pullbacks. She speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe's Space Race | Bloomberg Tech: Europe 7/10/2026
title_cn: 欧洲的太空竞赛|彭博科技：欧洲 7/10/2026
summary:
In this episode of Bloomberg Tech: Europe, Bloomberg’s Tom Mackenzie launches into Europe's efforts to develop its burgeoning space economy.  SpaceX has shown the might of American space technology - and financial muscle - with its record-breaking IPO. Bloomberg Tech: Europe looks at where Europe is lacking in the space race, and how it can secure space autonomy.

"Bloomberg Tech: Europe" spotlights the biggest names and trends shaping the region's technology ecosystem as the global competition heats up. This monthly, 30-minute magazine-style show features in-depth interviews with top technology leaders, as well as major investors and policymakers - giving you a compelling A to Z of the most consequential innovations, opportunities and challenges.  (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: The surge in tech campaigns on public transit is spurring satirists from New York to London to create their own versions.

title: Sovereign AI May Mean State-Backed Models and Political Tensions
title_cn: 主权人工智能可能意味着国家支持的模式和政治紧张局势
summary: Washington wants in on the artificial intelligence gold rush.

title: Big Tech Doubles Debt Load to $350 Billion in AI Spending Spree
title_cn: 大型科技公司在人工智能方面的支出狂潮，债务负担翻了一番，达到 3500 亿美元
summary: The largest builders of artificial intelligence data centers have doubled their debt load in the last five years, turning to borrowing to finance an unprecedented spending spree they claim is needed to transform the economy.

title: Spain's Cuerpo: 'Window Of Opportunity' For Joint EU Debt
title_cn: 西班牙库尔波：欧盟联合债务的“机会之窗”
summary: Spanish Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo said he sees a chance to revisit the idea of joint borrowing within the European Union, despite initial skepticism to a proposal he made on the subject this week from member states such as Germany and the Netherlands. Cuerpo made the comments in an exclusive interview with Bloomberg Television's Oliver Crook on Friday. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Mexico, Peru Open Door To Restoring Relations After Bitter Political Fight
title_cn: 墨西哥和秘鲁在激烈的政治斗争后打开恢复关系的大门
summary: Keiko Fujimori’s looming inauguration as Peru’s next president is opening a path to repair the Andean nation’s relationship with Mexico, after Lima broke off diplomatic relations last year.

title: Congo Tax Agency Seals Glencore Mine Offices in Payment Dispute
title_cn: 刚果税务局因付款纠纷查封嘉能可矿山办公室
summary: Tax authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo sealed off offices belonging to a Glencore Plc-owned copper mine, escalating a row over payments allegedly owed to the state.

title: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over But Talks on Deal to Continue
title_cn: 特朗普表示伊朗停火已经结束，但协议谈判仍将继续
summary: President Donald Trump said the US would continue talks with Iran but considers the ceasefire between the countries to be over.

title: DHS to Create Its Own Airline for Round-The-Clock Deportations
title_cn: 国土安全部将创建自己的航空公司，提供全天候驱逐出境服务
summary: The US government is moving ahead with plans to create its own fleet of government-owned aircraft to bolster deportations.

title: UK Police Launch Murder Inquiry After Death of Former Conservative Lawmaker
title_cn: 英国警方在前保守党议员去世后展开谋杀案调查
summary: Police launch murder inquiry after death of Ann WiddecombeBy David Hughes, Press Association Political Editor

title: New York City Stares Down a Care Crisis as Haitian TPS Expires
title_cn: 海地 TPS 到期后，纽约市面临护理危机
summary: The end of US work authorizations for hundreds of thousands of Haitians is threatening to leave short-staffed nursing homes, hospitals and home-care agencies with even fewer caregivers.

title: Iran Supreme Leader to Hold Memorial Ceremony for Slain Father
title_cn: 伊朗最高领袖将为遇害父亲举行追悼会
summary: Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei will hold a memorial ceremony for his predecessor and father Ali Khamenei on Friday, his office said, sparking speculation that the Islamic Republic’s top official could make his first public appearance since the war.

title: Qatar Blocks Defense Joint Venture Between VW, Israeli Firm
title_cn: 卡塔尔阻止大众与以色列公司之间的国防合资企业
summary: Qatar’s sovereign wealth fund vetoed a deal for Volkswagen AG to make vehicles supporting Israel’s Iron Dome mobile air-defense system, a plan intended to save hundreds of jobs at one of the German car manufacturer’s sites.

title: Reform UK Decries ‘Smear Campaign’ After Fresh Finance Probe Report
title_cn: 改革英国谴责新金融调查报告后的“抹黑运动”
summary: Reform UK’s deputy leader Richard Tice said his party was being subjected to a “smear campaign” after a newspaper reported that London police are investigating a £500,000 ($671,000) donation.

title: Trump Won’t Sign Housing Bill But Will Allow It to Become Law
title_cn: 特朗普不会签署住房法案，但将允许其成为法律
summary: President Donald Trump said he would not sign a bipartisan housing bill in protest over lawmakers declining to approve a separate voter-ID bill he has prioritized.

title: Hungary Unveils Agency to Recover Lost Orban-Era Assets
title_cn: 匈牙利成立机构以追回丢失的欧尔班时代资产
summary: Hungary’s government unveiled plans for a new investigative authority with the power to prosecute suspects and recover misappropriated public assets, fulfilling one of Prime Minister Peter Magyar’s key anti-corruption pledges.

title: German Lawmakers Approve Health Bill With Drug-Price Cuts
title_cn: 德国立法者批准医疗法案并降低药品价格
summary: German lawmakers approved a comprehensive overhaul of the country’s statutory health-insurance system that forces drug-price reductions on pharmaceutical companies as part of a wider drive to cut billions of euros in costs.

title: Erdogan’s NATO Gift of Revolvers Runs Into Europe’s Gun Laws
title_cn: 埃尔多安向北约赠送的左轮手枪违反欧洲枪支法
summary: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s parting gift to the NATO leaders he hosted this week was a revolver, and an accidental expose of squeamish European attitudes to firearms.

title: Turkey, Egypt Block LGBTQ Cruise Entry Over ‘Moral Values’
title_cn: 土耳其、埃及以“道德价值观”为由阻止 LGBTQ 邮轮入境
summary: Turkey and Egypt have blocked a cruise ship carrying more than 1,900 LGBTQ tourists from entering their ports, with the former citing a supposed threat to Turkish society.

title: Democrats Rush to Replace Platner as GOP Rejoices Over Divisions
title_cn: 共和党因分歧而欢欣鼓舞，民主党人急于更换普拉特纳
summary: Graham Platner’s political demise has set off an unprecedented scramble to find a replacement to take on Republican incumbent Senator Susan Collins, with at least six Democrats entering the wide-open field and less than four months until the November midterms.

title: Burnham Clears Final Hurdle to Becoming Next UK Premier
title_cn: 伯纳姆扫清了成为下一任英国首相的最后障碍
summary: Andy Burnham will replace Keir Starmer as Britain’s next prime minister, after securing the support of enough Labour MPs to prevent a rival challenger.

title: US Says Iran Talks to Continue Despite Hormuz Skirmishes
title_cn: 美国表示，尽管发生霍尔木兹小规模冲突，伊朗谈判仍将继续
summary: Talks between the US and Iran over a permanent peace deal are continuing, according to a American official, despite two days of clashes that threatened to shatter an already fragile ceasefire.

title: Hundreds Of Labour MP's Back Andy Burnham For PM
title_cn: 数百名工党议员支持安迪·伯纳姆竞选总理
summary: Andy Burnham's path to becoming the next UK prime minister looks certain after a vast majority of Labour MPs formally nominated him to be the next party leader. At the end of the first day of the party's leadership contest to replace Keir Starmer, 322 of 403 Labour members of Parliament voted for Burnham. Responding to the confirmation of the vote number, Burnham said he was "deeply grateful" for the support he's received from party colleagues, saying it "reflects a shared belief that Britain needs a new approach to politics." Bloomberg Opinion's Rosa Prince joins Caroline hepker and Rachel Evans on Daybreak Europe to discuss.' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Again Warns Russia Not to Use Nuclear Arms Against Ukraine
title_cn: 中国再次警告俄罗斯不要对乌克兰使用核武器
summary: China has again told Russia not to even consider using a nuclear weapon against Ukraine, according to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

title: Falling Israel Support Shapes US Political Battles Ahead
title_cn: 以色列支持率下降影响美国未来的政治斗争
summary: Americans’ shifting views are influencing foreign policy debates in both parties

title: Here’s Who Could Replace Graham Platner on the Maine Ballot
title_cn: 谁可以取代缅因州选票上的格雷厄姆·普拉特纳
summary: Maine Democrat Graham Platner’s withdrawal from the race for a US Senate seat unleashed a scramble by a handful of candidates who have little time to build support ahead of a convention the party is planning before a July 27 deadline to confirm a name on the ballot.

title: EU to Allow Ukraine to Buy UK Weapons With its €60 Billion Loan
title_cn: 欧盟允许乌克兰用600亿欧元贷款购买英国武器
summary: The European Union is set to allow Ukraine to purchase British weapons with the €60 billion ($69 billion) it’s loaning the war-torn nation for defense procurement.

title: Turkey Seeks to Offload Russian Weapon to Secure F-35 Access
title_cn: 土耳其寻求卸载俄罗斯武器以确保 F-35 进入
summary: Turkey is seeking Russia’s consent to transfer air defense systems it bought from Moscow to a third country, an effort aimed at clearing the way for the purchase of US-made F-35 fighter jets.

title: Indonesia Police-Military Tensions in Focus After Jakarta Raids
title_cn: 雅加达袭击事件后，印度尼西亚军警紧张局势成为焦点
summary: Indonesian officials sought to play down signs of a rift between security agencies after police raids in Jakarta brought several alleged graft cases into public view and drew a senior prosecutor into a widening controversy.

title: Peru Tests the Limits of Trump’s Anti-China Stance
title_cn: 秘鲁考验特朗普反华立场的极限
summary: President-elect Keiko Fujimori seems unwilling to ditch her father’s legacy of ties to Beijing.

title: Indonesia’s Prabowo Hosts Ex-Thai PM Thaksin for Danantara Talks
title_cn: 印度尼西亚普拉博沃接待泰国前总理他信参加达南塔拉会谈
summary: Former Thai premier Thaksin Shinawatra met Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto in Jakarta in his first high-profile overseas appearance since his release from jail, confirming his continuing role as an adviser to sovereign wealth fund Danantara.

title: Debt Fragmentation Is Costing EU 'Dearly,' Spain's Cuerpo Says
title_cn: 西班牙财政大臣库尔波表示，债务碎片化正在让欧盟付出“惨重”代价
summary: Spanish Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo discusses the idea of joint borrowing within the European Union, saying there is a "new window of opportunity" despite  initial skepticism to a proposal he made on the subject this week. “Fragmentation is costing us dearly," Cuerpo tells Bloomberg's Oliver Crook. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Will Andy Burnham Deal With Donald Trump?
title_cn: 安迪·伯纳姆将如何应对唐纳德·特朗普？
summary:
How will Andy Burnham deal with Donald Trump?

Political author Steve Richards tells Mishal Husain Britain's prime minister in waiting will be sure to turn on the charm. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Minister Defends US Pharma Deal Following Calls to Scrap It
title_cn: 英国部长在呼吁废除美国制药交易后为其辩护
summary: A trade deal with the US that increases the price Britain pays for new medicines will help patients and not cause hundreds of thousands of additional deaths, according to UK Technology Secretary Liz Kendall.
